title: eupatorium purpureum subsp. maculatum
intro: Eupatorium purpureum subsp. maculatum, commonly known as spotted Joe Pye weed, is a perennial plant native to North America that belongs to the sunflower family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Eupatorium purpureum subsp. maculatum have been associated with its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive issues, providing numerous health benefits. Eupatorium purpureum subsp. maculatum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
